title: Musk thanks Micron for chips, and builds a $55bn fab to replace it
excerpt: Memory chips are the oil of the AI boom, and right now they are hard to get. So when Elon Musk used Tesla’s quarterly call to publicly thank Micron, it was worth noticing. Suppliers rarely get shout-outs. Scarce ones do. Micron recently gave Tesla a “significant allocation” of memory on “reasonable terms,” Musk said, according […]
content_html:
Memory chips are the oil of the AI boom, and right now they are hard to get. So when Elon Musk used Tesla’s quarterly call to publicly thank Micron, it was worth noticing. Suppliers rarely get shout-outs. Scarce ones do.

Micron recently gave Tesla a “significant allocation” of memory on “reasonable terms,” Musk said, according to Bloomberg. That matters, because memory is in short supply and prices are high. The global build-out of AI has soaked up chips and left other industries scrambling.

The thank-you, though, sat oddly next to Musk’s longer game. He is quietly building the capacity to not need Micron at all.

Enter Terafab

The project is called Terafab, a joint effort between Tesla and SpaceX to make computing and memory chips at, in Musk’s words, unprecedented scale. A research fab is going up at Giga Texas, a roughly $3bn start able to turn out a few thousand wafers a month.

The full build-out could run from $55bn to $119bn, CNBC reported, with Intel signed on to help design and fabricate.

Musk was blunt about the reason. Terafab is “not to get leverage over our chip suppliers,” he said. “It is to ensure that we have enough chips down the road.” The industry, he argued, is not growing fast enough in logic or memory to meet his needs “unless we also make chips ourselves.”

Not even a top customer

There is a humbler truth underneath. For all Tesla’s fame, it is not a memory heavyweight. Neither Tesla nor SpaceX ranks among Micron’s top 10 customers, according to Bloomberg data.

Tesla also lacks the chequebook of the hyperscalers locking up supply for years. Micron did not respond to a request for comment, though it has every reason to keep a marquee name happy.

So the gratitude and the fab are two sides of the same worry. Tesla needs chips it cannot reliably buy, from a supplier whose bigger customers come first, in a market where Micron’s value has soared.

Thanking Micron today and building Terafab for tomorrow is not a contradiction. It is the plan. Whether a car company and a rocket company can also become a chipmaker is the $119bn question.

Published July 23, 2026 - 2:25 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.en: Published July 23, 2026 - 2:25 pm UTC
sentences.zh: 发布时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:25（世界标准时间）

content_html_zh:
存储芯片是人工智能繁荣的石油，但目前却很难获得。 因此，当埃隆·马斯克利用特斯拉的季度电话会议公开感谢美光科技时，值得注意。 供应商很少受到批评。 稀有的。

据彭博社报道，马斯克表示，美光最近以“合理的条件”向特斯拉提供了“大量内存分配”。 这很重要，因为内存供应短缺且价格昂贵。 人工智能的全球扩张已经吸收了芯片，并让其他行业陷入困境。

然而，这句感谢与马斯克的长期游戏相比显得很奇怪。 他正在悄悄地建立根本不需要美光的能力。

进入 Terafab

该项目名为 Terafab，是特斯拉和 SpaceX 的联合项目，用马斯克的话说，其规模是前所未有的，旨在制造计算和存储芯片。 德克萨斯州 Giga 正在建设一座研究晶圆厂，启动资金约为 30 亿美元，每月可生产数千片晶圆。

据 CNBC 报道，全部扩建费用可能从 550 亿美元到 1190 亿美元不等，英特尔已签约帮助设计和制造。

马斯克直言不讳地透露了原因。 他表示，Terafab“不会对我们的芯片供应商施加影响力”。 “这是为了确保我们未来有足够的筹码。” 他认为，该行业在逻辑或内存方面的增长速度不够快，无法满足他的需求，“除非我们自己也制造芯片”。

连顶级客户都算不上

下面还有一个更卑微的事实。 尽管特斯拉名气很大，但它并不是一个内存重量级产品。 根据彭博社的数据，特斯拉和 SpaceX 均未跻身美光科技的十大客户之列。

特斯拉还缺乏多年锁定供应的超大规模企业的支票簿。 美光科技没有回应置评请求，尽管它有充分的理由让这个大名鼎鼎的名字高兴起来。

所以感恩和美好是同一个担忧的两个方面。 在美光科技价值飙升的市场中，特斯拉需要无法可靠地从一家大客户优先的供应商那里购买芯片。

今天感谢美光和为明天建设 Terafab 并不矛盾。 这就是计划。 汽车公司和火箭公司是否也可以成为芯片制造商，这是一个价值1190亿美元的问题。

发布时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 2:25（世界标准时间）
